response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack engineer with legitimate distributed-systems and backend experience, but the profile falls far short of a CTO role at an agentic AI fintech company on virtually every executive dimension. His entire career is individual-contributor work — no evidence of managing, scaling, or developing engineering organizations. His AI/agent exposure is limited to a brief mention of MCP tooling at a role started only weeks before the resume was written, with no production multi-agent systems, no LLM evaluation methodology, and no fine-tuning experience on record. Fintech infrastructure depth is superficial: one developer-level role at an investment-adjacent company with no credit systems, payment flows, or regulatory compliance experience.
strengths: Solid high-throughput distributed-systems background from Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second with low-latency async I/O
Broad polyglot full-stack experience across TypeScript, Node.js, D, C++, mobile, and embedded systems demonstrating adaptability
Early awareness of agentic/MCP tooling and event-driven architectures at current role, showing alignment with the direction of the space
concerns: Zero engineering leadership track record — no team management, no org scaling, no architectural domain ownership beyond personal side projects

AI/agent production experience is effectively absent — the single MCP mention is from a role started weeks prior with no shipped production agent systems, no LLM infrastructure, and no evaluation pipelines
No fintech infrastructure depth — credit risk, working-capital allocation, real-time exposure modeling, payment flows, and multi-jurisdiction compliance are entirely missing from a 17-year career
